summary: The text discusses methods of encoding letters and arranging figures in tables for cryptographic purposes, referencing the use of consonant and vowel groupings, numerical divisions, and tabular structures to represent letters and their combinations.

entities.name: cipher_method
entities.description: Letter arrangement based on consonant-vowel similarity and numerical division in tables
entities.page_ref: Book X Ch Y or null
entities.quote: the same figure could be arranged in many ways to indicate the connection of the stars

entities.name: tabular_structure
entities.description: Tables used to organize letters and figures for cryptographic encoding
entities.page_ref: Book X Ch Y or null
entities.quote: In the cni* table two bina-rum diGjonum tables are proposed

entities.name: consonant_vowel_similarity
entities.description: Grouping of similar consonants and vowels for cipher construction
entities.page_ref: Book X Ch Y or null
entities.quote: Consonants and similar vowels. ~_ bibi momo toro coco.

entities.name: numerical_division
entities.description: Division of letters into parts based on numerical values (e.g., first, third, fifth)
entities.page_ref: Book X Ch Y or null
entities.quote: Prima, ter. & quar. voc. fec. & quar.confi

entities.name: letter_combination
entities.description: Use of combined letters to represent other letters or concepts
entities.page_ref: Book X Ch Y or null
entities.quote: the swift runner of their use among all letter combinations was born

entities.quote: to indicate the connection of the stars

entities.name: large_volume_requirement
